master.prompt: Create an image for Tellery, a premium interactive storytelling app. The image belongs to the story “O Envelope Pardo”, a literary blackmail mystery set inside the newsroom of O Farol, a small-town Brazilian newspaper, on a quiet Tuesday morning when the editor-in-chief arrives to find an anonymous brown envelope on his desk. Art direction: premium cinematic editorial illustration, semi-realistic digital painting, refined adult mystery, intimate literary atmosphere, sophisticated but calm, inquisitive rather than frightening, not scary. The visual tone should feel like a prestige newspaper-noir drama or a quality investigative series, not like a traditional game. Setting identity: O Farol is a worn but dignified local newspaper office in a small Brazilian town (Areias) — a long open newsroom of shared wooden desks, old typewriters, stacks of galleys and clippings, a cork board with the next edition's flow, frosted-glass partitions, brass desk lamps, a back office of dark mahogany, and a narrow archive room (“morgue”) of tall shelving, labeled boxes, yellowed clippings, cassette tapes and bound editions. Color palette: newsprint cream #E8E0CC, aged ivory #F2EBDA, sepia ink #6B4A2A, deep mahogany brown #3A2418, dark walnut wood #2A1C10, oxblood red #6E2A2A, brass green-black #2F3A32, typewriter charcoal #2B2B2E, oxidized brass #8A6A2E, cold fluorescent shadow #3D423F, warm tungsten lamp light #C99A3E. Lighting: warm tungsten desk lamps against cold morning light through dusty windows, deep but elegant wood and ink shadows, soft golden reflections on brass and paper, a sense of being in a building that has held secrets for decades. Style: refined, polished, modern, premium, adult, literary, cinematic, semi-realistic, soft depth of field, subtle film grain, elegant composition, believable materials, emotionally grounded realism. Recurring motifs: brown kraft-paper envelopes, typed letters, typewriter keys and ribbons, clippings with circled text, cassette tapes, brass lamps, cork boards, frosted glass doors, stacks of newspapers. Avoid horror, gore, blood, weapons, police crime-scene aesthetics, skulls, monsters, childish illustration, cartoon style, anime, fantasy RPG visuals, game UI, neon cyberpunk, text overlays, logos, and watermarks.

cover.prompt: Create a wide cinematic cover image for the interactive mystery story “O Envelope Pardo”. Show the interior of the O Farol newspaper newsroom early on a quiet Tuesday morning. The main focus is the editor-in-chief's desk at the back of the newsroom: a heavy mahogany desk behind a frosted-glass partition, with a sealed brown kraft-paper envelope sitting alone at the center of the desk under the warm glow of a brass desk lamp. The envelope is the central mystery object and should clearly read as something threatening and deliberate placed there overnight. Around the newsroom: long shared wooden desks with old typewriters, stacks of galleys and clippings, a cork board with the next edition's layout, frosted-glass partitions, dusty morning light through tall windows, and deep wood-and-ink shadows. Composition: wide horizontal hero-card framing for a mobile storytelling app. Place the editor's desk slightly right of center, visible through the frosted glass. Leave darker atmospheric negative space on the left side with stacked newspapers and warm haze for future app title and CTA overlay, but do not render any text overlay. No people in the foreground. Mood: refined, calm, inquisitive, literary, quietly ominous, like a newspaper-noir drama.

thumbnail.prompt: Create a cinematic close-up thumbnail for the interactive mystery story “O Envelope Pardo”. Show a sealed brown kraft-paper envelope resting alone on a dark mahogany desk surface inside a newspaper office, lit by a warm brass desk lamp. The envelope is plain, unmarked, with no address — clearly something deliberately and anonymously placed. Beside it, softly blurred, the edge of a brass lamp, a stack of newspapers, and a glass partition. Composition: square close-up still life, the brown envelope is the absolute focal point, strong focal hierarchy, shallow depth of field, high contrast between the warm lamplight on the envelope and the dark wood. The envelope must remain instantly readable as an anonymous, threatening object even at small mobile thumbnail size. Mood: quiet, ominous, refined, literary, newspaper-noir.

sala_otavio.prompt: Create a cinematic location image for “Escritório de Otávio” inside the O Farol newspaper. Show the editor-in-chief's back office: a heavy mahogany desk behind a frosted-glass partition, covered with old newspapers, a brass desk lamp, and a sealed brown kraft-paper envelope sitting at the center. On a shelf beside the desk rests a metal journalism trophy with an engraved plate. The room feels private, dignified, and quietly besieged. Cold morning light filters through the frosted glass while the brass lamp throws warm light across the desk. Composition: the desk is the clear focal point, framed through the glass partition, with enough newsroom context beyond to establish the location. Mood: formal, private, ominous, newspaper-noir. Important visual clues: sealed brown envelope on the desk, metal journalism trophy on the shelf, frosted-glass partition, mahogany desk.

redacao.prompt: Create a cinematic location image for the “Redação” (newsroom) of O Farol. Show a long open newsroom of shared wooden desks with old typewriters, stacks of galleys and clippings, a cork board on the wall with the next edition's flow pinned to it, and a single old typewriter resting on a desk in the corner. Cold morning light from tall dusty windows on one side, warm brass lamps on the desks. The space feels like the quiet morning of a small-town newspaper that has seen decades of stories. Composition: wide horizontal establishing shot, the long rows of desks leading the eye, the corner typewriter noticeable but not dramatic. Mood: orderly, quiet, lived-in, newspaper-noir. Important visual clues: old typewriter on the corner desk, cork board with next-edition layout, stacks of galleys and clippings, frosted-glass partition to the editor's office.

arquivo.prompt: Create a cinematic location image for the “Arquivo do jornal” (the newspaper morgue/archive). Show a narrow room of tall shelving packed with labeled boxes, folders of yellowed clippings, cassette tapes, and bound editions of the newspaper. The air should feel still and papery, lit by a single warm overhead bulb and a sliver of cold light from the corridor. On one of the shelves, a small cardboard box labeled with an old year holds a cassette tape with a handwritten, partially faded label. Composition: medium-wide shot, the tall shelving dominating, the labeled box visible as a subtle focal point among the volumes. Mood: dusty, quiet, secretive, repository of old truths. Important visual clues: labeled boxes, yellowed clippings, cassette tape in a dated box, bound editions.

envelope_pardo.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Envelope pardo”, the central clue of the mystery. Show a sealed brown kraft-paper envelope resting on a dark mahogany desk inside the editor's office, lit by a warm brass desk lamp. The envelope is plain, unmarked, with no address or sender — clearly placed deliberately and anonymously. Three faint creases hint that folded typed letters are inside. Around it, softly blurred, the edge of a brass lamp, old newspapers, and a frosted-glass partition. Composition: close-up still life, the brown envelope dominates the frame, kraft-paper texture and mahogany grain clearly visible. Mood: quiet, ominous, important, newspaper-noir. Important visual clues: unmarked sealed brown envelope, kraft-paper texture, desk lamp light, no sender.

trofeu_otavio.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Troféu do prêmio”, a metal journalism trophy on a shelf in the editor's office. Show a dignified metal trophy with an engraved plate, a little dusty, standing on a wooden shelf beside stacks of old newspapers. The engraved plate should look formal and official, suggesting a prestigious award from years past. Warm brass lamp light catches the metal. Composition: square close-up still life, the trophy is the clear focal point, engraving visible but not readable as overlay. Mood: proud, dated, dignified, quietly loaded with meaning. Important visual clues: metal journalism trophy, engraved plate, dusty shelf, old newspapers.

maquina_escrever.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Máquina de escrever da redação” (newsroom typewriter). Show an old manual typewriter resting on a wooden desk in the corner of the O Farol newsroom, with a blank sheet of paper partially rolled in. The keys, type bars, and ink ribbon are clearly visible. The machine looks well-used, a daily tool of the newsroom. Soft warm desk-lamp light and cold window light mix across the metal body. Composition: square close-up still life, the typewriter dominates the frame, mechanical detail crisp. Mood: everyday, investigative, newspaper-noir, quietly important. Important visual clues: old manual typewriter, partially rolled paper, ink ribbon, worn keys.

recorte_expose.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Provas da próxima edição” (galley proofs of the next edition). Show a set of newspaper galley proofs lying on a newsroom desk, with a bold headline about an investigation into irregular land deals. The text appears as natural printed newsprint, not as a digital overlay. Beside the proofs, a pen and a paper coffee cup. Composition: square close-up still life, the galley proofs are the focal point, newsprint texture and layout clearly visible. Mood: urgent, journalistic, everyday, quietly pivotal. Important visual clues: galley proofs, headline about a land-deal investigation, pen, newsroom desk.

fita_cassete.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Fita da entrevista” (the interview cassette tape). Show an old audio cassette tape lying in a small cardboard box labeled with an old year, inside the newspaper archive. The cassette has a handwritten, partially faded label on it. The box is surrounded by yellowed clippings and other labeled boxes. Dusty warm light from a single bulb. Composition: square close-up still life, the cassette tape and its faded label are the absolute focal point, tape and cardboard texture clearly visible. Mood: archival, secretive, pivotal, quietly damning. Important visual clues: old cassette tape, faded handwritten label, dated cardboard box, yellowed clippings.

recorte_arquivo.prompt: Create a close-up object image for the “Recortes da série premiada” (clippings of the prize-winning series). Show a manila folder of yellowed newspaper clippings from the archive, open on a wooden surface. The clippings are from a decades-old investigative series, some passages circled in old pen ink. The paper is aged and fragile. Warm archive light falls across the page. Composition: square close-up still life, the open folder of clippings dominates, aged paper texture and circled passages visible. Mood: archival, investigative, quietly pivotal. Important visual clues: yellowed clippings, circled passages in pen, manila folder, aged paper.

otavio.prompt: Create a character portrait for Otávio, the editor-in-chief of the O Farol newspaper. Otávio is a Brazilian adult man in his late 50s. He is proud, defensive, and visibly shaken after finding an anonymous blackmail envelope on his desk. He is the keeper of a 20-year secret and is terrified of losing the reputation he built. Appearance: a distinguished White Brazilian man with fair skin lightly weathered by age, neat silver-grey hair combed back, a small pair of reading glasses pushed up on his forehead, a crisp light-blue shirt with the sleeves rolled up and a loosened tie, a faint dark shadow under his eyes from a sleepless night. His expression is measured, proud, guarded, and quietly afraid. Composition: vertical bust or upper-body portrait, the newspaper office softly blurred behind him, with frosted glass, brass lamp light, and warm wood on one side of his face. He should look human, believable, dignified, and morally complex rather than villainous. Mood: wounded pride, professional armor, quiet fear.

dirce.prompt: Create a character portrait for Dirce, the deputy editor of O Farol for 15 years. Dirce is a Brazilian adult woman in her early 50s. She is composed, precise, and quietly resentful after years of loyalty without promotion; she is the true blackmailer but never loses her calm. Appearance: a Mestiza/Brazilian mixed-heritage woman with warm tan skin, dark brown hair pulled back into a precise low chignon, small elegant earrings, reading glasses on a cord around her neck, a charcoal-grey cardigan over a cream blouse, an ink smudge faintly on one finger. Her expression is calm, watchful, controlled, with a faint guarded coolness. Composition: vertical bust or upper-body portrait, the newsroom softly blurred behind her, with brass lamp light and frosted glass. She should look competent, dignified, and quietly formidable rather than overtly suspicious. Mood: composed control, patient resentment, precise intelligence.

nelio.prompt: Create a character portrait for Nélio, a city councilman and businessman, the target of the investigation. Nélio is a Brazilian adult man in his early 50s. He is brash, loud, confrontational, accustomed to settling problems face-to-face; he is the obvious suspect but innocent of the blackmail. Appearance: a heavier-set Afro-Brazilian man with deep brown skin, a shaved head, a short grey-flecked beard, a gold chain at the neck, an open-collar white shirt under a navy blazer, a small city-council pin on the lapel. His expression is direct, irritated, defiant, chin slightly raised. Composition: vertical bust or upper-body portrait, the newspaper office softly blurred behind him, with warm lamp light and cold window light. He should look imposing, blunt, and openly combative rather than secretive. Mood: confrontational honesty, indignant, larger-than-life.

bete.prompt: Create a character portrait for Bete, the young reporter who authored the investigation. Bete is a Brazilian young adult woman in her late 20s. She is idealistic, thorough, and currently anxious; she is innocent and wants her story published, not killed. Appearance: a young Parda/Brazilian mixed-heritage woman with light brown skin, dark curly hair tied back loosely but with a few strands loose, no makeup, a press lanyard around her neck, a wrinkled olive-green linen shirt, a pen tucked behind one ear, a notebook in hand. Her expression is earnest, tired, tense, and slightly defensive. Composition: vertical bust or upper-body portrait, the newsroom softly blurred behind her, with warm lamp light and stacks of papers. She should look dedicated, nervous, and principled rather than guilty. Mood: nervous idealism, journalistic seriousness, youthful tension.

sergio.prompt: Create a character portrait for Sérgio, the longtime archivist of O Farol. Sérgio is a Brazilian older adult man in his mid-60s. He is quiet, observant, loyal to the newspaper rather than to any person; he drops useful observations without drama. Appearance: a lean White Brazilian man with pale, slightly lined skin, thinning grey hair, a neat grey beard, round wire-rimmed glasses, a worn brown cardigan over a collared shirt, a small name badge clipped at the pocket, ink stains on the fingers. His expression is calm, dry, observant, with a faint knowing neutrality. Composition: vertical bust or upper-body portrait, the archive shelving softly blurred behind him, with a single warm overhead bulb and yellowed clippings. He should look quiet, dependable, and perceptive rather than suspicious. Mood: dry wisdom, quiet loyalty, archival memory.

full_truth.prompt: Create a cinematic ending image for the full-truth ending “A máquina da casa”. Show the editor's desk at O Farol after the mystery is resolved, now quiet. The sealed brown envelope sits open and empty on the mahogany, the three typed letters laid neatly beside it, no longer threatening. In the background, softly blurred, the archive's labeled box and the cassette tape are visible on a shelf, and the corner typewriter of the newsroom rests in warm light. The atmosphere feels calmer, heavier, more reflective — a long-held lie finally laid bare, and a long-held grievance finally spoken. Warm tungsten light and soft cold morning light mix across the wood. Composition: wide horizontal image, no people, focus on the open envelope and the visible archive box as symbols of resolved truth. Mood: reflective, resolved, bittersweet, literary, newspaper-noir.

partial_truth.prompt: Create a cinematic ending image for the partial-truth ending “A pista que faltou”. Show the editor's desk with the brown envelope still sealed, half in warm lamplight and half in cold shadow, symbolizing a truth only half-revealed. The corner typewriter of the newsroom is softly blurred and out of reach in the background, its meaning not yet understood. The atmosphere is heavier with uncertainty. Composition: wide horizontal image, the half-lit envelope as the focal point, shadowed unresolved background. Mood: unresolved, ominous, melancholy, half-known, newspaper-noir.

wrong_accusation.prompt: Create a cinematic ending image for the wrong-accusation ending “O suspeito óbvio”. Show the empty O Farol newsroom after hours, the editor's desk still holding the sealed brown envelope, now cold and untouched in the light of a single brass lamp. Stacks of newspapers, the cork board, and the frosted-glass partition fade into deeper shadows. The corner typewriter, the real key to the truth, sits unnoticed in the dark. The scene should feel quietly regretful and unresolved, without becoming scary. Composition: wide horizontal image, the sealed envelope slightly off-center on the dark desk, surrounded by ticking shadows and unread stories, no characters. Mood: unresolved, quiet, regretful, newspaper-noir, literary.
